page: Vultr voc-s-16c-128gb-2560s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring an AMD EPYC-Rome processor with 16 physical cores and 16 vCPUs on an x86_64 architecture. The CPU operates at a base frequency of 2.0 GHz and utilizes a shared allocation model. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled, consisting of a 2560 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 2927.78, placing it in the top 25% of tested hardware. Multi-core performance is moderate, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 46301.39, which falls within the middle 50%. The CPU supports instruction sets such as AVX, AES, SHA_NI, BMI2, and FMA, though it lacks AVX-512 capabilities.

This server offers a high-density memory and storage profile relative to its shared CPU allocation. The shared CPU model represents a potential tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained, dedicated compute performance, but it enhances qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive and storage-heavy tasks. The combination of 128.0 GB of RAM and 2560 GB of NVMe SSD storage makes this configuration suitable for database hosting, caching layers, memory-intensive data processing, and general-purpose workloads requiring substantial local storage.
description: Vultr voc-s-16c-128gb-2560s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server configured with an AMD EPYC-Rome processor, offering 16 vCPUs and 16 physical cores on a shared CPU allocation model. The system features 128.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core, alongside a bundled 2560 GB NVMe SSD. In performance benchmarks, the server achieves a strong single-core stress-ng score of 2927.78, ranking in the top 25%, and an average multi-core score of 46301.39. This hardware profile provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive and storage-heavy workloads. The server is well-suited for database hosting, caching layers, and data processing applications that benefit from large memory capacities and fast local storage, despite the shared CPU allocation tradeoff.

tagline: A memory-optimized server combining 128 GB of RAM and 2.5 TB of NVMe storage with strong single-core performance.
